entry.title: SAP Commerce Cloud and Solr Upgrade
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I’ve noticed a warning in the SAP Commerce Cloud Portal that says: "To minimize the impact on your operations, update your Commerce solution to the latest available version. For more information, see the Supported Releases page and Update Releases page. To ensure compatibility with the composable storefront libraries, see the Composable Storefront Compatibility Matrix page.".

Our current SAP Commerce Cloud version is 2211.20, and we're using Solr 8.x. We came across an article on SAP Help that mentions that the 2211.20 version will be valid until September 10, 2024.

We have a few questions:

  1. If we don't upgrade Solr and SAP Commerce Cloud patch version to 2211.27, will it affect our build and deploy process?
  2. How much time do we have to complete these upgrades?

Thanks!


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAP Commerce Cloud and Solr Upgrade
entry.content:
Hello there!

Indeed the SAP Commerce Cloud 2211.20 will be available only until the date you mentioned as per Update Releases - Update Releases Timeline. After that date all builds created with this version will fail with the exception described in the KBA 3242156 - Build not possible for EOMM versions of SAP Commerce Cloud, but deployments with already created builds will still be available. You can find this and more information also in Using an EOMM Version. 

Regarding the Solr 8.x version, it will still be compatible until the patch 25 of SAP Commerce Cloud 2211, but we keep the suggestion of always upgrading to the latest release available, which today is the patch 27. This means that you will need to also upgrade your Solr version to 9.x as it is the only compatible version with SAP Commerce Cloud 2211.27.

You have until the date of End Of Mainstream Maintenance (for the 2211.20 version is September 10, 2024) to upgrade to a higher version. If you wish, you can upgrade to SAP Commerce Cloud 2211.25 so you have time to prepare the Solr upgrade to 9.x as it is also compatible with the 8.x, but don't forget its EOMM date: January 21, 2025! 

Kind Regards,
Wesley

entry.title: Re: SAP Installation Server and Microsoft Global Secure Access
entry.content:
Well finally, we managed to complete the task and update the machine over the Internet using Global Secure Access. We had to enable anonymous access on the share following the Domain file share with anonymous access - Microsoft Q&A. 

We edited the group policy for SAP Installation Server and apart from the group policy setting the following configurations:

  • "Accounts: Guest account status" – Enabled.
  • "Network Access: Let Everyone permissions apply to anonymous users" - Enabled

You have to set:

  • "Network access: Do not allow anonymous enumeration of SAM accounts and shares" - Disabled
  • "Network access: Shares that can be accessed anonymous." - Specify the shared folde names of SAP installation server

In this manner and after applying the GPO and restarting SAP Installation Server (these settings tweak HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE), I restarted the AWUS service and after authenticating on Global Secure Access, SAP upgraded the installation package to the latest version.

entry.title: Re: List of POD Plugin Events for SAP Digital Manufacturi...
entry.content:
Q1: Where do I find a list of all POD Plugin Events I can use for SAP Digital Manufacturing custom development? 

A1: The Plugin Events available for SAP Digital Manufacturing (SAP DM) are detailed in the POD Plugin Developer's Guide, accessible on the Help Portal. Additionally, examples of event usage and guidance on creating custom events can be found in the SAP GitHub repository referenced in section 4 of the guide.

Q2: Are there any events that are not listed in the POD Plugin Developer's Guide on the Help Portal?

A2: No, all available events are listed on the SAP Help Portal. The event presented in Section 4 of the GitHub repository is a custom event created as an example for reference. You can create your own events and subscribe to them in various POD plugins.

entry.title: Restricting EWM Inspection lot generation - The usage of Badi /SCWM/EX_QM_INSP_RELEVANCE
entry.content:
Requirement: System should not generate EWM Quality inspection lot when Vendor is sending same Supplier /SAP batch. If the supplier or SAP batch has ever been inspected, EWM should skip generating inspection lot and post GR into unrestricted stock type.

Problem Addressed/ Standard Scenario: There is no SAP standard functionality which allows to skip inspection lot creation based on above scenario when its product having Supplier/ SAP batch already undergone Usage decision. Standard SAP keeps generating inspection lot or add into common inspection lot using ' Inspection Lot Summary. But there is no workaround in standard to avoid generating 'Inspection Lot' if SAP/Supplier batch is already inspected.

Process: During receiving against Purchase Order either from Fiori or RFUI, system creates inspection lot for inspection type '01' for corresponding Supplier/SAP batch. Latter, result recording and Usage decision is conducted for the required supplier/ SAP batch with acceptance or rejection. 

Solution: Since there is no standard functionality available to address the above requirement, SAP has provided Badi /SCWM/EX_QM_INSP_RELEVANCE  which can be used to prevent inspection lot creation according to the business requirement.( e.g. for a Product and its batch is already inspected) .This BAdI is available for both decentralized and embedded landscape.

During first time SAP batch receiving 0000000140, system creating corresponding QI inspection with Q4 stock type. 

In same vendor lot/ SAP batch of the required product is received again. standard SAP would either create a new inspection lot or with inspection lot summary it will club this batch into already created inspection lot( Not inspected) using 'Inspection Lot Summary' as below

 

 Badi Implementation: Using SAP Badi /SCWM/EX_QM_INSP_RELEVANCE, it is possible to add a logic of stopping generation of inspection lot if Supplier/SAP batch has already been inspection. 

We build a logic reading QALS table if Product/Batch is already inspected in any of the plants, avoid creation of inspection lot generation using changing parameter of Badi.

In IS_QINSP_DATA importing parameter in Badi, customer can read many fields & can build their logic on custom requirement( E.g Avoid creation of inspection of certain document type or item type ) using CV_NOT_RELEVANT as influencing parameter.

Post Badi Implementation: Post Badi implementation, system allowed to skip inspection lot creation for Same Vendor/SAP batch combination in below example.

In below example, a shipment is received already received and inspected for Supplier Batch VEND006/ SAP batch 0000000140.

UD is completed.

In QALS table, inspection is marked with UD.

Receiving the same Vendor/ SAP batch combination, badi allowed to skip inspection lot creation.

On top of that, customer has to implement SAP note:3200795 - Stock type remains in quality when inspection is set to not relevant via BAdI. Even if you implement the badi, system would skip the inspection lot creation, but the stock of inbound delivery items remain in  'Q4' stock type. The above note fixes the issue of stock type change from Q4 to F2 at GR or at Warehouse task creation

Thanks for reading. All test is performed in S/4 HANA 2022 Sandbox Embedded EWM system

The blog is Co-Authored by  @prem_swaroop   my colleague from SAP labs India.

entry.title: Re: New End User File Upload in SAP Analytics Cloud QRC3 2024
entry.content:
@gerd_schoeffl , @JefB 

In my testing today I found out this file upload method is able to upload data to parent nodes also without any extra job settings. Its tricky now because sometimes users copy paste data from other excel to prepare the data and there is no way to reject parent node data by enabling any settings. Also the upload template should have a sample negative number else the measures get recognized as integer and would truncate decimals later. So consciously the measure column needs to be set as number. Once transported the upload job query when edited is not able to retrieve the original upload template used while setting up the query so the upload template used should be kept handy for any future edits to the job. 

Also I hope in future release the upload status message will show records loaded as this was very useful for users to validate the load. Now this information is available in model data management console which end users might now have access to.

 

Nikhil

entry.title: Familiarize with Revtrac fundamentals functions
entry.content:
This Article is aimed for beginners / learners associated with Revtrac tool.  Let us explore few fundamental functions.

Creating users in revtrac:

  • Login to solman/Revtrac installed system  via user id and password
  • Login to revtrac console ( tcode : /n/rsc/rt01). To avoid entering tcode every time , add this to your favorites)
  • In console – go to organization
  • Click edit and add a new entry
    •  

    •  

  • Value is, U=0, A=1, R = U and then email address in lowercase. (exception for PI U=2)
  • Save the entry and go back one screen and activate changes.

Adding peer review:  These requests come in form of snow request where the new or existing user needs to be a peer reviewer as well. This basically allows team members to approve their transports for code change/functionality change etc. This is a not requested frequently. 

  • In console – go to organization
    • Click each tab under users to reach organization structure
      • Edit à add an entry à add: team name (there are already populated and can also found in Snow ticket) , Position – Peer for peer review, userID, leave the standin box, Full name )
      • Save the change and go back one screen
      • Click on activate changes and update the snow ticket.
      •  
      • Approving RTR (Rev-Trac request) :   
        • You’re alerted with an email.  You can click the link in the email request or login to URL -> enter the rtr number

        https://hostname.xxxx.com/revtrac/SID/dashboards

        Steps:

        • Open the RTR# (by using one of the above)
        • Click on Migrate to prod tab à Click yes to subsequent pop up and post completed you will the task bar will move forward to in production tab. That means the approval is completed. Resultà for example : 2475 ( it is approved and ready for production move)

        •  

        • Exceptions: OOPS error: if there is a oops error, make sure you reach out to ops
        • Updating mass update to users.    After our change in domain, we have a requirement to change email addresses of all users. So here are the steps to convert them.

        • Yes, the email address needs to change in the user table in Rev-Trac…

          Yes, there is an easier way…!

        • First, back up the entire configuration like below:

        • Save the output to something like “SID-Config - 031523.dnl”. we are backing up the whole configuration in case anything goes wrong (so we have a backup of existing one)

          Then, do the following:

        • Then, look at the next two screenshots:

        • Select only the users and download to a file named something like “SID-Users - 021523.dnl

          Now edit the notefile and replace the domain using find and replace.

          Once done do the similar steps only for users and this time upload the file instead of download.

        • Thank you for taking time to go through this article.

entry.title: Re: Data Architecture with SAP – Data Lake
entry.content:
We have a requirement to store JSON documents in the database and execute queries on them to retrieve specific parts of the file, instead of retrieving the entire JSON document into application memory for processing.
As part of our analysis, we found that SAP HANA Cloud Data Lake can store and retrieve files.
Our questions are: What are the capabilities of Data Lake and the Data Lake Relational Engine? Do we need the Data Lake Relational Engine to execute queries on documents stored in the Data Lake?
Our main concern is the significant cost associated with the Data Lake Relational Engine.

entry.title: Re: How to expand default memory size for SAP S/4HANA Clo...
entry.content:
Hi,

At this point, not many customers accumulated a large quantity of data in the system to consider archiving. This 100 GB Licensed Memory is a threshold to be used in those objects to be considered as archiving. This is a starting point. If this threshold is too small, customers can raise a ticket to our ops team to request a change with their reasons.

One more thing to consider is that we are using a HANA DB here. This database avoided a lot of zero values to be stored in the database. So 100 GB could store a lot of data comparing with RDBMS.

Regards,

George

entry.title: Re: OData and MDK: I can't access the value of a property...
entry.content:
I see where you have set the design time target for your detail page to include the $expand=TIPOAUDITORA but keep in mind that this is used only in the editor to know what properties are assumed to be available when using the Object Browser.  At runtime for these bindings to work the default binding object passed into the page is assumed to have been expanded and available.  

Assuming you are coming from a list page you need to make sure the list target has the same $expand in the target so the expanded properties are available on the detail page.

entry.title: BEx Analyzer and Analysis for Office Comparison & Migration from BW to BI
entry.content:
comparison between these tools:

Existing users who are familiar with BEx Analyzer will find the transition to Analysis relatively smooth. With a familiar MS Office UI, comprehensive feature set and overall improved design, unlocking value out of your enterprise DWH should be made even easier.

Below are the screenshots that shows the look and feel in terms  between Bex and AO and also shows to login to AO.

Initial screen once open the respective tool as below:

Once click on the below options, it allows to connect to the respective system:

Once connected to the system, then open the query and the tool asks for the Prompt/Variable screen as shown below. once the prompt values are given click on the OK button. 

Then it displays the output as below:

We are done with look and feel of AO, now lets go with Migration of WB from BW to BI platform.

Migrating Analysis for Office Workbooks from BW to BI Platform:

We are in process of moving a lot of Analysis for Office (AfO) workbooks from a SAP BW environment to the SAP BusinessObjects BI Platform.
Open the workbook you want to migrate, connect to the BW system and refresh the WB. Display the Design Panel, go to the Components tab and select your workbook. Right-click to Log Off.

Now right-click the workbook again. Select Replace System.

now see the Replace System menu, where you can select a new system.

Initially this only shows the SAP systems that are defined in your SAP Logon. Click the button in the left bottom corner to login to the BI Platform.

Now the OLAP connections from the BI Platform are also selectable. Choose the connection you want to use.

now select the data source  in the Components tab you’ll see the new System Alias.

Now you can Refresh the workbook and/or Save it to the BI Platform.

Now login to the BI Launchpad of respective system and locate the report and schedule the same. 

Thank you for reading my blog. Comments and feedback are welcome!

entry.title: Order Based Planning: Finite heuristic not generating planned order
entry.content:
Hello Experts, 

We have setup Order base planning process using SAP7 and SDI for one of our business process. The integration of master data and txn data from ECC to IBP is working with use of SDI.

We are using Finite Heuristic method in the Supply planning run. It is generating the receipts for the externally procured item without any issue. 

However, the finite heuristic is not generating any receipts against demands ( sales order and forecast) at the manufacturing plant for the inhouse produced items. The valid resource with available capacity and the PDS data are successfully integrated from ECC to IBP.

In the Supply run profile , we have not set any constraints ( capacity, lot size etc.) and the source of supply for PDS is enabled. I am expecting a planned order, but it does not plan any thing nor indicates any clue in the log.

Appreciate if you can pl share your inputs.

Subh

entry.title: How to implement the additional sections of XML file in the SAF - T Report?
entry.content:
How to implement the additional sections of XML file in the SAF - T Report?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to implement the additional sections of XML file...
entry.content:
The functionality of SAF - T Report is that as of now, the scope defined by legal authorities would require Header section in the XML file and this is generated from the standard implementation. Based on further scope from legal authorities, partner will need to implement the additional sections of XML file. The details of partner implementation and enablement is not part of the current scope.

You can check this in the documentation link:  https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BUSINESS_BYDESIGN/2e30d84496214461ad763692c3a0b4a5/bfd13d243d874fa1999c566f967fa948.html#prerequisites

So for the requirement of implementing the additional sections of XML file in the SAF - T Report, this is not possible currently as there is no scope for the additional sections. Even though if you try to add some fields to the XML file through BADI but as per the existing system setup, there is no provision to add new fields, only updating the existing fields is possible via BADI. 

Also our recommendation is to post this as an enhancement idea over our Influence platform. The same is monitored by our Product Management team, and posts are considered for next releases of the system.

You can access the Influence forum in the link below:

https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/886

entry.title: Is there any security risk when running scripting with an older SAP GUI version?
entry.content:
Good day.

Is there any security risk  when running scripting with an older SAP GUI version? Is there a way to enforce minimum GUI version otherwise users will not be able to run scripts?

Kind regards


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Is there any security risk when running scripting wit...
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you for reaching out to SAP Community.

  • The most recent changes to the SAP GUI security parameters were made at least two years ago. Therefore, it doesn't seem logical to enforce versions. Instead, you should ensure that users running outdated versions receive upgrades. If there is a requirement to enforce a version, there is the SAP Note 748424 - "Evaluation of SAP GUI versions and patches" that provides a sample report for doing so.
  • Furthermore, there are no known security issues with SAP GUI Scripting. The important point is that scripts can only perform actions that users are capable of, but they can do it more quickly (the scripting simulates the UI interactions performing by the users). 
  • SAP recommends SAP GUI Scripting rather other third-party software since it has a very secure technology, especially when compared to browser-based UIs that can be automated through tools without offering any additional security measures.

I hope above comments can help you to be more aware of the security risks regarding SAP GUI scripting.

entry.title: ABAP Cloud Sessions at SAP TechEd in 2024
entry.content:
Hey Everyone!

That time of year is here again, SAP TechEd 2024 is just around the corner! 

SAP TechEd is the leading tech conference for all developers, IT professionals, business experts, tech visionaries, innovators, and leaders eager to gain in-depth knowledge of SAP’s solutions, platforms, and technologies, along with insights into their future direction. It also offer an opportunity to network and connect with peers and SAP experts. 

SAP TechEd in 2024 will kick off with a free, engaging virtual event accessible to everyone, followed by SAP TechEd on Tour stops all around the world for a unique opportunity for more in-depth, in-person learning experiences. The series of local on-site events will be organized in partnership with and hosted by SAP User Groups and SAPinsider. Learn more about the virtual experience here.

The choice is yours: Join us virtually for the first event in the series, SAP TechEd Virtual 2024! 

📌Save the date and register now!
📅October 8 - 9, 2024

At this year's SAP TechEd, you will have the opportunity to attend a variety of sessions and formats - such as lectures, jumpstarts, and roadmaps - organized into six (6) content tracks that are aligned with your business needs, covering SAP technologies, products, and solutions:

  • Digital Transformation with Cloud ERP (DT)
  • SAP Business Technology Platform – General (XP)
  • Application Development and Automation (AD)
  • Data and Analytics (DA)
  • Integration (IN)
  • Artificial Intelligence (AI)

 The ABAP sessions at this year's SAP TechEd event will be centered around "Clean Core Extensibility powered by ABAP Cloud and Generative AI". Attendees can look forward to a range of lectures (incl. roadmaps) and jump-starts on clean core development and transformation with ABAP Cloud and GenAI for all SAP S/4HANA editions, both cloud and on-premise. Below is an overview of the SAP TechEd Virtual 2024 sessions related to clean core extensibility and ABAP Cloud. 

📚Access the full session catalog of SAP TechEd Virtual 2024



Keynotes

KEY100CEST & KEY100EDT | SAP TechEd in 2024 Launch - Where ideas get real
[Executive Keynote] At SAP TechEd, experts, developers, IT leaders, and our SAP Community gather to learn, share, and build together. Hear firsthand from SAP Board Members Juergen Mueller and Muhammad Alam how innovations in SAP Build solutions, SAP Business AI, SAP BTP, and other solutions across our end-to-end portfolio are revolutionizing the tech world.´
Speakers: 
      • Juergen Mueller, Chief Technology Officer, SAP
      • Muhammad Alam, SAP Product Engineering, SAP
Schedule: 
      • 📅 Tuesday, Oct 8 | 🕛 9:00 AM - 10:30 AM CEST [KEY100CEST]
      • 📅 Tuesday, Oct 8 | 🕛 4:00 PM - 5:30 PM CEST [KEY100EDT]

DEV101A & DEV101B | Keynote recap + Community welcome
[Developer Keynote] Join the Developer Advocates as they welcome the SAP Community to SAP TechEd, provide a short recap of the key announcements, and share insights into what it means for developers. 
Speakers: Developer Advocates, SAP 
Schedule: 
      • 📅Tuesday, Oct 8 | 🕛 10:30 AM - 10:55 AM CEST [DEV101A]
      • 📅Tuesday, Oct 8 | 🕛 5:30 PM - 5:55 PM CEST [DEV101B]

DEV100A  & DEV100B | Developer keynote [deconstructed]: Keeping the promise
[Developer Keynote Deconstructed] Hear from the developer advocates for a quick injection of news, recaps, and deep-dive demos covering various announcements from the event as well as other fun stuff. 
Speakers: Developer Advocates, SAP 
Schedule: 
      • 📅Tuesday, Oct 8 | 🕛3:30 PM - 3:55 PM CEST [DEV100A]
      • 📅Tuesday, Oct 8 | 🕛9:00 PM - 9:25 PM CEST [DEV100B]

Track Overviews 

AD100 – Unlock SAP Build and ABAP Cloud interoperability to keep your core clean
[Track Overview, 45 min] Discover a new approach to building extensions while keeping your core clean. Learn how SAP Build and ABAP Cloud interoperability work together to foster fusion development. Whether you prefer pro-code or low-code tools, discover how to seamlessly integrate them to build extensions tailored to your needs. Learn from experts about best practices, innovative solutions, and practical applications that can enhance your development processes and drive business growth. 
Speakers: Dr Alexander Rother (SAP), Thomas Volmering (SAP), Björn Schulz (REWE)
Schedule: 📅 Tuesday, Oct 8 | 🕛 6:00 PM - 6:45 PM CEST

DT100 – Unlocking the intelligent enterprise: Cloud ERP innovations for the future
[Track Overview, 45 min] Explore the future of cloud ERP, focusing on the latest innovations and strategies driving digital transformation. Delve into the technological backbone of SAP's vision for cloud ERP, covering in-app extensibility, integration with SAP Business Technology Platform, AI-powered automation, and the "suite-first" approach. Learn the benefits of a clean core and its role in enabling composability, empowering businesses to adapt and evolve, and get insights into this year’s cloud ERP track sessions.
Speakers: Jan Gilg (SAP)
Schedule: 📅 Tuesday, Oct 8 | 🕛 10:30 AM - 11:15 AM CEST

Lectures

📅Tuesday, Oct 8

AD105 – Enhance your fusion team collaboration with ABAP Cloud and SAP Build 
[Lecture, 25 min] Learn how to take advantage of interoperability between SAP’s low-code and pro-code solutions. Discover how you can use the graphical modeler in the development environment of SAP Build Code for the ABAP Cloud development model and how your ABAP Cloud projects can be integrated into the lobby of SAP Build solutions. 
Speakers: Dr. Anne Keller (SAP), Tim Back (SAP)
Schedule: 📅 Tuesday, Oct 8 | 🕛  7:00 PM - 7:25 PM CEST

AD107 – Boost your coding efficiency: Explore Joule’s ABAP Developer capabilities
[Lecture, 25 min] Discover Joule's ABAP developer capabilities. Hear about detailed use cases, the generative AI road map, and insights on how these advancements can boost your productivity as a developer in your daily tasks. 
Speakers: Dr. Jasmin Gruschke (SAP), Sebastian Baskovich (SAP)
Schedule: 📅 Tuesday, Oct 8 | 🕛  7:30 PM - 7:55 PM CEST

📅Wednesday, Oct 9

AD106 – Clean core extension of SAP S/4HANA with the latest ABAP Cloud features
[Lecture, 25 min] ABAP Cloud is the comprehensive development model for building cloud-ready business applications, services, and extensions on both SAP Business Technology Platform, SAP S/4HANA Cloud, and SAP S/4HANA, helping ensure clean core compliance by definition. Learn about the latest features offered in ABAP Cloud and how generative AI can boost the development efficiency. 
Speaker: Volker Drees (SAP)
Schedule: 📅 Wednesday, Oct 9 | 🕛 7:00 PM - 7:25 PM CEST

DT201 – Extensibility for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
[Lecture, 25 min] The extensibility portfolio for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition consist both on-stack and side-by-side extension techniques. In this lecture you will learn how on-stack key user extensibility can be used to develop a custom business object together with an application in SAP Build Apps. 
Speaker: Dr. Thomas Schneider (SAP), Alda Dollani (SAP)
Schedule: 📅 Wednesday, Oct 9 | 🕛 2:30 PM - 2:55 PM CEST

DT200 – Clean Core Extensibility for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
[Lecture, 25 min] Learn how to manage your custom, on-stack extensions in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition with the ABAP Cloud development model following clean core principles. Find out about the new guidelines for clean core ABAP development in a well-known, three-tier extensibility model and how to handle transformation options for existing custom code to ABAP Cloud. Get an outlook of upcoming generative AI support and hear our plans for ABAP test cockpit to support governance of your clean core developments.
Speaker:  Olga Dolinskaja (SAP), Thomas Fiedler (SAP)
Schedule: 📅 Wednesday, Oct 9 | 🕛 3:00 PM - 3:25 PM CEST

Jump-Starts

AD180 – Build SAP Fiori Apps with ABAP Cloud powered by Joule's ABAP Developer capabilities
[Jump-Start, 25 min] Learn how to develop transactional SAP Fiori apps in ABAP Cloud using the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model supported by generative AI. You will also learn about features such as business events for loosely coupled integration scenarios and read-only treeviews for displaying hierarchical data. 
ABAP Cloud is the comprehensive development model for building clean core compliant apps, services, and extensions on SAP Business Technology Platform and SAP S/4HANA, in the cloud and on-premise. 
Speaker: Carine Tchoutouo Djomo (SAP)
Schedule: 📅 Tuesday, Oct 8 | 🕛 7:30 PM - 7:55 PM CEST

DT280 – Building Clean Core Extensions with ABAP Cloud for SAP S/4HANA Cloud
[Jump-Start, 25 min] Learn how to speed up the implementation of SAP S/4HANA Cloud extensions with ABAP Cloud using two new ABAP Development Tools (ADT) based generators for adding extension fields and for building custom UI services on top of business objects that have been released by SAP. 
Speaker: Andre Fischer (SAP)
Schedule: 📅 Tuesday Wednesday, Oct | 🕛 3:30 PM - 3:55 PM CEST

I hope this overview gives you a few more reasons to join us at SAP TechEd 2024.

We look forward to meeting and interacting with you virtually at SAP TechEd Virtual and in person at the local events in West Palm Beach (US), Melbourne (AUS), Copenhagen (DK), Birmingham (UK), and Wiesbaden (DE).

In the meantime, you can check out Devtoberfest by SAP TechEd 2024 and watch the replays of last year's events, SAP TechEd Virtual in 2023 and SAP's Devtoberfest in 2023.

Stay tuned - and see you there!

entry.title: Re: What in ILX does the "Accomplishments" menu do (and h...
entry.content:
@Kristian_Unell, I believe the Accomplishments link in the Activities mini menu refers to Learning Accomplishments, which is a feature in the Learning module that was released several years ago. Seeing that option in the Activities menu means that the Access Learning Accomplishments workflow has been added to the USER Learning security role assigned to the test user that you were using in your test system.

To confirm in your test system, view the permissions assigned to the User Learning security role(s) that your company uses. In Learning Admin > System Admin > Security > Role Management, there are two default User roles in called DEFAULT USER and LEARNING_USER. In addition, during implementation, our partner created a copy of the default User role and named it something similar, so you may need to review all of the User roles to find the one that has the "Access Learning Accomplishments" workflow assigned (the workflow lives in the Learning group). Note: If your company has added the Role field to the User entity configuration, you can view the People > Users record of the test user to see what security role is assigned to that test user.

Once you determine which User role contains the Access Learning Accomplishments permission, remove that permission from the role. Log out of Learning and then login again to confirm that the Accomplishments link has been removed from the Activities menu on the ILX.

CAVEAT: I would strongly recommend you review all of the User roles that are being actively used in Production to determine whether this workflow has been assigned to any of them. It may be that the workflow was never removed from the User role in Test but was not actually included in the User role in Production.

Good luck,
Mary Katherine

entry.title: MTO Production order execution, with variable GR qty for sales order with lot charge
entry.content:
The business process is transforming customer supplied semiconductor wafers into individual shipping components.

Key elements:

  1. Sales orders are billed as a lot charge, with a sales order unit of one. (It is this way due the customer requirements and purchase order)
    1. Regardless of the resulting good parts, the customer pays for processing all of them.
  2. The associated MTO production order needs to be quantity of one, as the stock quantity needs to match the sales order qty.
  3. However, the lower-level production orders, have quantities greater than one, as all the stock is at the individual component level. (this is typical with wafers, as each wafer has a known variable good qty)
    1. As a result, we have to “write off” 201 the excess stock and only post quantity of one to the MTO production order.

The ask: does SAP provide any functionality to manage this type of business process, without a bunch of transactional work arounds?

entry.title: Is it possible to mass update Equipments with the Migration Cockpit?
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support,

By using transaction IE05 (Change Equipment - Mass Change), we noticed that it's not possible to update the same fields of multiple equipments with different values for each field.

Considering this, we'd like to know the following:

  • Is it possible to mass update equipments with the Migration Cockpit (Migrate Your Data app) as we've described?

Thanks in advance!

Best regards,
Expert team


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to mass update Equipments with the Mig...
entry.content:
Dear experts,

In regards to the app's functionalities, it's not possible to mass update or change equipments with the Migration Cockpit.

The Migrate Your Data app (Migration Cockpit) was designed only to migrate the initial data required on an SAP S/4HANA system. Further data loads besides the first one(s) or mass changes and updates to already existing data aren't the purpose of the app, so, consequently, it also can't update the data maintained for equipments in the system. For mass changes, the respective business area application (or transaction) must be used, as IE05.

More information about the Migration Cockpit's designed purpose and standard functionalities can be read in the KBA below:

  • 2684818 - SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit usage for mass processing data or as interface

I hope this answers your question!

Best regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: How does Half day holiday displayed in table TFACS
entry.content:
Hi Shlomi, np. better late then never :).

Just to be sure, you need to have the same values as in MON01..MON02 as half days? 

MON01..MON12 have each 31 chars, for each day one char: 1-working day / 0 - notworking day. For me half-day is the same as working day - so every '1' in the MON01...MON12 columns stays for working day, either half or full.

Do you need to have a difference between first-half or second-half day? If not, I would just duplicate the MON01...MON12 1:1, and when calculating with the duplicated columns, just divide by 2. 

Martin

entry.title: One of my CDS CQL statements doesn't change the backing postgre-sql database
entry.content:
In my Core Data Services project (running locally but with --hybrid switch to 'connect to' a BTP destination and connected to a postgre-sql database also running locally in a docker container), I have several cql statements, three of which are UPDATE statements.  All but two of the cql statements 'work', including one of the UPDATE statements.  However, two of the UPDATE statements don't seem to update the backing postgre-sql database.

All of the statements are using '.run()' directly on the cds object (none are creating transactions).  My understanding is that cds.run() creates and then (on success) commits a transaction.

Below is the code being run for one of the UPDATE statements that doesn't update postgre-sql.  The two SELECT statements are there to help me diagnose.  When the code runs, the first SELECT shows null for submissionBegunAt.  After the UPDATE runs, the second SELECT shows the date that was assigned during the UPDATE run, for submissionBegunAt.

However, after all the code runs, the database doesn't reflect the changes.

CODE THAT 'DOESN'T WORK'

const markBegunAt = async () => {
let affectedGroup = await _closureVariables.cds.run(
SELECT(_closureVariables.entities.Roles)
.where(
{
request_formNumber: lineItem.request_formNumber,
moduleRole_module_ID: {in: lineItem.roles.map(r => r.moduleRole_module_ID)},
moduleRole_ID: {in: lineItem.roles.map(r => r.moduleRole_ID)}
}
)
);

console.log(affectedGroup);
//const tx = _closureVariables.cds.transaction();
//_closureVariables.cds
try {
const result = await _closureVariables.cds.run(
UPDATE(_closureVariables.entities.Roles)
.set({submissionBegunAt: new Date().toISOString()})
.where({
request_formNumber: lineItem.request_formNumber,
moduleRole_module_ID: {in: lineItem.roles.map(r => r.moduleRole_module_ID)},
moduleRole_ID: {in: lineItem.roles.map(r => r.moduleRole_ID)}
})
);

console.log(result);
//await tx.commit();
}
catch(exception) {
console.log(exception);
//await tx.rollback();
}


affectedGroup = await _closureVariables.cds.run(
SELECT(_closureVariables.entities.Roles)
.where(
{
request_formNumber: lineItem.request_formNumber,
moduleRole_module_ID: {in: lineItem.roles.map(r => r.moduleRole_module_ID)},
moduleRole_ID: {in: lineItem.roles.map(r => r.moduleRole_ID)}
}
)
);

console.log(affectedGroup);

And below is the code for the UPDATE that's 'making it to the backing postgre-sql database'

CODE THAT WORKS

UPDATE(_closureVariables.entities.Roles)
.set({status: "A"})
.where({
request_formNumber: req.data.requestId,
moduleRole_ID: req.data.roleId,
moduleRole_module_ID: req.data.moduleId
})
 
I don't see any substantive difference in these pieces of code.
 
I appreciate any help!

entry.title: SAP Datasphere- نمذجة البيانات
entry.content:
SAP Datasphere تحقيق أقصى استفادة من بيانات الأعمال
 
 نموذجك الأول وتقريرك الأول

. هو حل متكامل لإدارة البيانات وتحليلها، يتيح للمؤسسات تنظيم وتجميع وتحليل بياناتها من مصادر متعددة في بيئة واحدة متكاملة

مما يوفر أدوات قوية لتصميم نماذج البيانات، وإنشاء تقارير تحليلية متقدمة وتنفيذ عمليات استخراج وتحويل وتحميل البيانات و يساعد المؤسسات على  اتخاذ قرارات مستنيرة بناءً على بيانات دقيقة وشاملة

Datasphere هذه المقالة موجهة للمبتدئين الذين يرغبون في معرفة كيفية استهلاك البيانات من

Datasphere  وستتبع هذه المقالة سلسلة من المدونات للحصول على فهم كامل  حول

نموذجك وتقريرك الأول

:  بدايةً، لنعرف ما هو منشئ البيانات. يقدم منشئ البيانات مجموعة من المحررات لإنشاء العناصر في طبقة البيانات مثل
  الجداول-
  العرض الرسومي-
SQL  عرض-
إنشاء هيئة جديدة-
  النموذج التحليلي-
 تدفق البيانات-
  البحث الذكي-
تدفق النسخ المتماثل-
 سلسلة المهام-

منشئ البيانات هو أداة تتيح للمستخدمين إنشاء وتعديل وإدارة مجموعة متنوعة من العناصر في طبقة البيانات. تشمل هذه الأدوات محررات متنوعة تساعد على تصميم الجداول، إنشاء العروض الرسومية ،  تحديد العلاقات بين البيانات، بناء النماذج التحليلية، وتنفيذ عمليات البحث الذكية

:الخطوة الأولى 

يمكنك استيراد الملفات, تقوم بتحديد ملف المصدر من جهاز الكمبيوتر الخاص بك. في هذه الحالة، الملف الذي قمت بتحضيره 

                                                                                                    :  الخطوة الثانية  

نبقي كل شيء كما هو ونضغط على نشر  وتعطيه اسمًا، في حالتنا، "طلب مبيعات" وتقوم بنشره.  النشريعني أننا نقوم فعليًا بإنشاء الجدول على قاعدة البيانات   

بعد نشر الجدول بنجاح، ستجد الجدول في نظرة عامة بمنشئ البيانات. دعنا نضغط عليه. وهكذا نصل إلى محرر الجدول

: الخطوة الثالثة 

نحتاج إلى تعيين معرّف "رقم طلب مبيعات"كحقل مفتاح، ثم نشر الجدول. لذا يمكننا الآن أيضًا عرض البيانات في المعاينة بعد نشر الجدول ستظهر هذه البيانات في أسفل الصفحة، ستستغرق ثانية، ثم يمكنك معاينة البيانات التي قمنا بتحميلها مباشرةً ,يتيح لك محرر الجدول تعريف الجداول ودلالاتها 

:الخطوة الرابعة

 الآن بعد استيراد الملف ونشر الجدول المحلي، يمكنك استخدامه لنمذجة إضافية. لنقم بإنشاء  عرض بسيط ليتم استخدامه  لإعداد تقرير بسيط . وهنا أيضًا، في نظرة عامة منشئ البيانات، نضغط على زر إنشاء عرض رسومي جديد

تظهر لوحة فارغة الآن، مع نافذة على اليسار لمستودعك وملفات المصدر. نفتح الجداول ونرى جدول طلب مبيعات الذي قمنا بتحميله للتو

لنقم بسحبه إلى اللوحة. والآن يظهر قسم الخصائص إلى يمين الشاشة. نضغط على خصائص المقطع. نعطيه اسمًا، مثل "روئية البيانات  لطلب مبيعات". نظرًا لأننا نريد استخدامه للتقارير، نحدد الاستخدام الدلالي لمجموعة بيانات تحليلية. تحتاج أيضًا إلى عرض الاستهلاك بحيث يكون مرئيًا للاستخدامات الخارجية. ستحصل على رسالة خطأ تفيد بأنه لم يتم تعريف أي مقاييس بعد. لذا، لنأخذها هنا: المبلغ الإجمالي، المبلغ الصافي، ومبلغ الضريبة، نميزها ونسحبها ببساطة إلى قسم المقايس

.الآن يمكنك حفظ ونشر هذا العرض. ولا تنس نشره لكي نتمكن من المتابعة

:الخطوة الخامسة

الآن أنت في الخطوة الأخيرة لإنشاء تقرير بسيط للتحقق من نموذج البيانات الخاص بك. نستخدم زر تبديل التطبيق في الزاوية العلوية اليمنى

SAP Analytics Cloud  للتبديل نذهب إلى 

نذهب إلى القصص، وننشئ  تقرير جديدة، يمكنك إضافة رسم بياني وستظهر نافذة منبثقة تطلب منك الاتصال بنموذج البيانات الخاص بك 

علينا تحديد مقياس, لقد حددنا ثلاثة مقاييس: المبلغ الإجمالي، المبلغ الصافي، ومبلغ الضريبة. لذا دعنا نأخذ المبلغ الصافي. نريد تقريرًا بناءً على منظمات المبيعات. الآن قمنا بإنشاء تقريرنا الأول. يمكنك حفظه. وبهذا نكون قد انتهينا من الجزء الأول 

بشكل عام، هناك خيارات مختلفة لاستهلاك البيانات  تقدم اتصالًا مباشرًا حيًا، وهذا ما رأيناه في مقالنا، ابق على اتصال مع المقالة التالية لتتعلم كيفية 

.الاتصال بمصدر بعيد وإضافة البعد الزمني

entry.title: Re: SAP ByDesign webservice : Query Employee Actual times
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

Hope you're doing well.

By checking this scenario further in the SAP Business ByDesign, I was able to find the webservice "Manage Employee Actual Times" in Communication Arrangement "Time Administration Integration".

This can be checked by following the steps below:

  1. Go to the Application and User Management work center.
  2. Access the Communication Arrangement work center view.
  3. Select the communication scenario named Time Administration Integration.
  4. Click the Edit button.
  5. Navigate to the Technical Data tab.
  6. Click the Edit Advanced Settings button.
  7. Select the Manage Employee Actual Times service in the Inbound Communications table, then click Download WSDL.
  8. Open the WSDL in a API testing software (such as Postman).

You mentioned that you are facing an error message, if you are using the WSDL from the steps above already, then please raise a case to SAP Support so we can evaluate the root cause of the error message.

Thanks and best regards,
Saulo.
SAP Product Support

entry.title: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance Virtual Summit 2024
entry.content:
More and more authorities are exploring the benefits of a fully digital reporting and electronic invoicing system. With multiple levels of regulations to adhere to, it is crucial for enterprises to stay up-to-date with the latest regulatory trends and resources to enable compliance. SAP can help you on this journey. 

We are excited to announce the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance Virtual Summit 2024, where you will have the opportunity to explore SAP's solution for electronic document processing and statutory reporting: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance.

We will discuss the solution's roadmap and best practices to assist you in complying with ever-changing legal regulations worldwide. Secure your spot and be part of a global conversation on how to achieve compliance excellence and drive your business forward with SAP. Learn more about the event here. 

SAP Document and Reporting Compliance Virtual Summit 2024
When: September 18 and 19

8:00 a.m. – 12:00 p.m. EDT

9:00 a.m. – 1:00 p.m. BRT

2:00 p.m. – 6:00 p.m CEST

5:30 p.m. – 9:30 p.m IST

Where: Virtually – join us from anywhere!

Agenda:

SAP Document and Reporting Compliance – today and on the roadmap

  • Learn more about SAP Document and Reporting Compliance and its upcoming innovations​
  • Receive information on the latest on the roadmap​
  • Understand what SAP’s strategy means for you as a customer
  • Learn how customers are succeeding with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance
  • Provide SAP with your individual feedback

Deep dive with experts – breakout sessions on:

  • Architecture and solution offering
  • Cross-applications within SAP
  • Customer and partner testimonials
  • ​What's new in Global Tax Management overall?​
  • Many other related relevant topics

Register here

entry.title: Understanding The Cost of Supply Chain Disruption
entry.content:
Supply chains are the backbone of many industries and businesses. Every part, from raw materials to operational technology, must arrive on time and meet the exact specifications to keep production running smoothly. When supply chain disruption strikes, the impacts are devastating. In 2021, during the height of post-COVID supply chain disruptions, the average cost of supply chain disruptions for an organization was $228 million according to Statista. 

Factors such as persistent inflation and high interest rates mean that supply chain risks are still alive and well today. According to Deloitte, the leading cause of supply chain issues in Central Europe was higher prices for commodities. This is because the majority of suppliers are private companies with finite resources, so, when inflationary pressures hit, their cash reserves are squeezed and they become less resilient against unforeseen disruption. 

In today’s economic landscape, how can companies gauge risk levels across their entire supply chain? Through financial health monitoring.  

By analyzing the financial statements of their suppliers, organizations understand whether their suppliers have the resources to withstand unexpected challenges. And they can take proactive steps to mitigate risk. At a broader level, companies can observe risk in portfolios or categories of suppliers and detect risk concentrations effectively.   

RapidRatings analysis of supplier financials across four key sectors in manufacturing shows where risk is heavily concentrated: 

  • Automotive: The bulk of the automotive supply chain is Transportation Equipment Manufacturing, making up about 18% of the supply chain. However,  RapidRatings data shows that 30% of suppliers in this sector are high risk or very high risk. 
  • Healthcare and Pharma: One of the most critical supplier sectors to the pharma and healthcare industries is Scientific Research and Development Services, as it drives and innovation and development in drugs and devices. Concerningly, 24% of these suppliers are at high risk or very high risk based on their ability to withstand financial pressure and other challenges.   
  • Aerospace and Defense: Aerospace Product and Parts Manufacturing is a vital segment in the aerospace and defense supply chain, yet 36% of suppliers have a high or very high risk of defaulting – representing a substantial level of exposure to disruption.  
  • Technology: Close to 20% of the semiconductor and other electronic component manufacturing sector are high or very high risk based on analysis of their financial health. 

By implementing comprehensive financial health monitoring, organizations can identify high risk suppliers and manage their exposure to disruption risk. RapidRatings’ FHR is a financial health assessment that provides ratings on private and public suppliers using financial statements, giving companies a comprehensive view of risk levels across their entire supply chain  

Understanding supplier’s financial health is not solely about avoiding or reducing exposure to risk, but about building strategic partnerships. Financial assessments allow you to partner with strong suppliers, increase longer-term commitments, and create mutual growth opportunities.  

In today's volatile economic climate, supply chain disruption is a constant threat. By prioritizing financial health monitoring and building robust supplier relationships, businesses can significantly reduce their exposure to risk and gain a competitive advantage.

entry.title: Re: GRC Tuesdays: Hidden Gems – SAP Watch List Screening for Simplified Due Diligence and Compliance
entry.content:
Hi @Henry_Banks, 

Thank you for your question.

As highlighted in the Supplemental Terms and Conditions: "The Customer is required to maintain an active license to the Mendel Verlag content during the entire Term of the Cloud Service". This is because SAP Watch List Screening uses third-party content provided by Mendel Verlag and that content packages are not included in the Cloud Service.

I trust this clarifies.

Kind regards, 

Thomas

entry.title: Re: How to create reports for Concur report ID and financ...
entry.content:
Hello,

The "Concur Integration Setup" App is for you to maintain the connection between your SAP Concur and SAP S/4HANA Cloud systems & through Concur Posting Documents Monitor App, you can monitor all documents imported into that system. This app displays the status of all documents, the payment status when possible, if attachments are available, and any customer modifications that were done. 

To find the expense report IDs, you can use the ‘Display Line Items in General Ledger’ app.
Here is the documentation. Use the filters to select the relevant company code, fiscal year, and posting date. This will display the line items in the general ledger that are associated with the specified Concur expense report ID. You can then view the details of the financial postings for each line item.

Thank you!
Cryssal

entry.title: Enable Email Automation in C4C
entry.content:
Hello Developers!!!

I hope you’re doing well.

This blog is all about to sent a specific C4C report on a certain interval to a list of recipients in the system. We would be using the standard feature Broadcast in Business Analytics work center.

Requirement

The Client team would like to receive the C4C licensed user's last log-on date on a monthly basis. The report should include the below fields.

1. Employee Name                                        3. Employee Email

2. Employee Technical Name                        4. Last Logon Date

Resolution

In order to achieve this; firstly we have to create a custom Data Source which can fetch the relevant connected data. To achieve this you can join the below Data Sources in the below format. 

1. Employee Master Data Source & User Subscription (DS1)

2. DS1 + User Logon Details (DS2)

Note: You can do all this in one Data Source; but as the data source was being used for other reports as well; we have created a new Data Source.

Once the data source is ready; you can use the final Data source (DS2) to create a report. Ensure you also set fixed selection to the below fields as below to reduce the data set & loading time of the report so as to get good performance.

Once you have finished the enhancement; you can start to creating selection and views to the report.

Note: The data is masked due to privacy concerns.

Later you can create a broadcast from Business Analytics -> Broadcasts.

 

In the detail screen you can add relevant details like below.

Once the format, report view & selection and frequency is set; you can save and close the broadcast. One of the most important this is that you have to assign the report to all the recipients business role else the broadcast would fail throwing access error.

Once that's done you can go to the Broadcasts listing screen and start the Broadcast like below.

Resolution

The above steps were implemented and later the broadcast has started working. Once the Broadcast is started you will receive an email from the SAP System with the report. Thus, the automation was achieved.

Hope you enjoyed the blog. We have a lot more coming, so do like and follow for more such content.

Bye bye!! 😊 

Ananthu R Biju

Senior C4C Consultant
